title: Pungent Aerated Canadian Onion Hash
description: A kid-friendly Canadian dish featuring a pungent yellow onion and a low-carb-friendly, al-dente-finish.

ingredients.name: Yellow onion
ingredients.notes: use a sweet onion like yellow or Vidalia for minimal tears

instructions: Melt the butter in a large cast-iron skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Sprinkle the salt and pepper over the onion and stir to combine.
Increase heat to medium-high and cook, stirring frequently, until the onion is caramelized and dark golden brown, about 15 minutes.
Add the bacon bits and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Push the onion mixture to one side of the skillet.
Crack in the eggs and cook, stirring constantly, until the eggs are almost set.
Use a spatula to gently stir the eggs into the onion mixture.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake at 375°F (190°C) for 12-15 minutes, until the top is golden brown and the eggs are set.
Remove from the oven and sprinkle with shredded cheese.
Return the skillet to the stovetop over low heat and cook, stirring occasionally, until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh chives if desired.
